summary: Executes the user defined csi graph.
description:
This block is customizable thus the settings and the type of the data ports can be changed using the block configuration dialog.
Also, local data port, blocks and links can be added.
For more information please read the following documentation.

controlPorts.Success: when the function returns a successful result.
controlPorts.Progress: when the function returns a intermediate result.
controlPorts.Error: when the function returns an error.

dataPorts.Call: the input data when the function is called.
dataPorts.Success: the successful data when Success control port is activated.
dataPorts.Progress:
the intermediate data when Progress control port is activated.
Can be filled by the implementation with links or by the graph engine with progress events.
There is a difference between the versions of the graph.
In graph version 1, the data returned is always a CSIExecGraphProgress (even when the progress is coming from the implementation, contained by the data property).
But in version 2, the implemention data is passed as is and the progress events are sent as CSIExecGraphProgress.
For more information please read the following documentation.
dataPorts.Error:
the error data when Error control port is activated.
Can be filled by the implementation with links or by the graph engine when an internal error occurs.
There is a difference between the versions of the graph.
In graph version 1, the data returned is always a CSIExecGraphError (even when the error is coming from the implementation, contained by the data property).
But in version 2, the implemention data is passed as is and the internal graph errors are sent as CSIExecGraphError.
For more information please read the following documentation.
dataPorts.NodeIdSelector: to override the node id selector when the function is called.
